examples: Serious dehydration can lead to cramps, chills and disorientation.
Suddenly the corridor of forest is gone. In its place is sheer disorientation.
Shock, seizures, tremor, disorientation, and coma may be seen in the late stages.
The contrast with the outside world was so extreme as to cause disorientation, dizziness.
The substance, while vital to all life, can, if inhaled in pure form by humans, cause dizziness, disorientation and even unconsciousness.
